Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: First I need to calculate the total duration for each case by comparing the earliest and latest timestamps Looking at the data cases 2003 and 2005 clearly stand out with multiday processing times while others finish within hours

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: For root cause analysis I notice both problematic cases involve high complexity and multiple document requests But there are differences case 2003 is handled by Adjuster Mike in Region A with a single repeated document request while case 2005 has Adjuster Lisa in Region B making three separate requests

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The resources and regions differ but the common thread seems to be high complexity leading to document requests that create delays

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: I should also check if specific resources or regions correlate with delays beyond just complexity The data shows both regions appear in delayed cases and different resources handle them so the primary driver seems to be claim complexity itself rather than specific resource or region factors

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: For recommendations I can suggest process improvements like prechecking documents for highcomplexity claims implementing SLAs for document requests and perhaps workload balancing for adjusters handling complex cases The key is addressing the document request bottleneck that appears in both delayed cases
